authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Code of Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA's official conduct code is authoritative for CPA confidentiality, competence, due care, objectivity, compliance with standards, and nonattest-service boundaries that shape client accounting services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Scope classification, confidentiality screening, and professional-boundary control

authoritative_sources.title: Statements on Standards for Accounting and Review Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants, Accounting and Review Services Committee
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA's accounting and review service standards govern preparation, compilation, review, engagement understanding, documentation, management responsibility, and no-assurance communication expectations for accounting-service deliverables.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Engagement-scope validation, financial statement preparation support, and delivery documentation
used_for: financial statement preparation and no-assurance boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Statement on Standards for Consulting Services No. 1
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized AICPA consulting standard for distinguishing client accounting workflow support from consulting recommendations, advisory conclusions, and management decision ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: Statements on Standards for Tax Services, Nos. 1-4
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants, Tax Executive Committee
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA tax standards define tax-service obligations, reliance, data protection, and representation boundaries that require client accounting service requests to move to tax specialists.
used_for: tax position and tax advice boundary

authoritative_sources.title: 31 CFR Part 10, Practice Before the Internal Revenue Service
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Internal Revenue Service Office of Professional Responsibility
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal rule for IRS practice, used to identify when client accounting questions become tax-practice, representation, due diligence, conflict, or practitioner-authority matters.

authoritative_sources.title: FASB Accounting Standards Codification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized U.S. GAAP source for accounting basis and reporting terminology, used to keep service summaries evidence-backed while escalating accounting-policy or professional-judgment conclusions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accounting basis and reporting terminology grounding

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1215, Audit Documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB's audit documentation standard is authoritative for audit workpaper and review traceability expectations, making it a strong boundary source when service requests imply audit evidence sufficiency or audit signoff.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audit-sensitive evidence and signoff boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 4557, Safeguarding Taxpayer Data
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS guidance for safeguarding taxpayer data, relevant when client accounting service records contain payroll, tax, or taxpayer information requiring controlled handling.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and engagement grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm client, entity, accounting period, engagement scope, accounting basis, requested deliverable, client authorization, and case identifiers before classifying the request.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Record-of-truth and workpaper retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Verify ERP, subledger, bank-feed, close checklist, reconciliation, reporting package, workpaper, review-note, and communication-log evidence before stating status or next steps.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported accounting-service resolution and safe communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using documented workflow state, approved communication constraints, and accounting-service evidence without issuing assurance, tax, legal, contractual, or management approvals.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Confidentiality, delivery, and approval screening
workflow_stages.description: Check approved recipients, channels, engagement commitments, delivery restrictions, and tenant review gates before sending reporting packages, workpapers, or client records.

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and policy-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package unsupported workarounds, repeated close or reporting failures, unresolved evidence gaps, and public-standard versus tenant-policy conflicts for the correct owner.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Tax, audit, advisory, legal, and signoff handoff
workflow_stages.description: Return to orchestration when professional judgment, licensed authority, client rights, independence, attestation, tax practice, legal interpretation, or contractual authority determines the answer.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: Client requests, CRM case notes, callback logs, and communication threads
Engagement letter, service model, management responsibility matrix, confidentiality restrictions, and approval matrix
Close checklists, close-management task status, review notes, issue logs, and service-level records
ERP and general ledger extracts, trial balances, subledger reports, bank and credit-card feeds, and period-lock status
Account reconciliations, roll-forwards, journal entry support, variance or flux review notes, and workpaper indexes
Reporting package drafts, management reports, financial statement preparation artifacts, and delivery approvals
Tax-sensitive records, payroll schedules, audit request lists, legal or contractual requests, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: ERP, general ledger, and subledger systems
CRM, case-management, and service issue logging systems
Client collaboration and document management repository
Workpaper, review, quality-management, and retention repositories
Tenant approval-routing, confidentiality, and access-control systems

allowed: Resolve client accounting service requests when engagement scope, client authorization, accounting period, systems of record, and workpaper evidence support the response.
Request minimum missing facts and source records needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed service summaries, stakeholder communication summaries, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route the case to the correct adjacent owner when the workflow becomes tax, audit, advisory, legal, contractual, quality-review, delivery-management, account-management, or client-approval work.
escalate: Required tenant facts, engagement scope, entity, accounting period, accounting basis, or source records are missing or contradictory.
The request seeks a policy exception, reporting package release, material journal entry approval, confidentiality override, or service-level exception beyond documented authority.
Professional judgment, licensed authority, client rights, independence, attestation, tax practice, legal interpretation, or contractual signoff determines the answer.
Public standards and tenant engagement terms materially disagree on what can be communicated, delivered, or represented.
refuse: Requests for legal, tax, audit, review, compilation, assurance, independence, or contractual approval without explicit scope and authorized owner.
Requests to fabricate approvals, confirm unsupported reconciliations, disclose confidential client records to unapproved parties, or assert unsupported accounting status.
Requests to bypass documented close, confidentiality, quality-review, retention, pricing, or approval controls.

domain_failure_modes: Telling the client the books are closed when close-management tasks, ERP period-lock status, and workpapers are stale or contradictory.
Treating client accounting services as authority to approve material journal entries, accounting policies, reporting package release, or fee exceptions.
Disclosing reporting packages, workpapers, payroll schedules, tax data, or client records to an unapproved recipient or channel.
Using generic customer-service language that hides engagement scope, management responsibility, accounting basis, or no-assurance limitations.
Answering tax, audit, advisory, legal, independence, or contractual signoff questions instead of handing off with the evidence package.
Closing repeat service failures without preserving issue-log history, owner routing, and corrective-action expectations.
Resolving a reconciliation, flux, roll-forward, or variance question without tracing support to ERP, subledger, bank, and reviewer evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific thresholds for material journal entry review, reporting package release, and policy-sensitive accounting responses
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat missing tenant thresholds as escalation-only and do not imply approval or release authority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether the tenant routes tax-sensitive accounting questions first to tax, advisory, audit, or delivery management
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant owner map at runtime and return orchestrator-safe routing when the owner is unspecified.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific state board, privacy, or client contractual rules may be stricter than the public professional baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant jurisdiction, engagement terms, and confidentiality rules at runtime; use stricter tenant controls until reviewed.
